title: FalsePositiveCase
description: A YAML-first fixture for false-positive control cases. High structural organization, self-reference, or continuity — but G (givenness) is absent or unestablished. If the theory promotes any of these to phenomenal status, the theory has failed.

g_score.description: G / givenness score. Should be 0.0 for all confirmed false positives. If > 0, the fixture is misclassified or the theory has failed.

g_established.description: Is there credible evidence for phenomenal appearance-from-within? false = control case.

structure_features.description: Structural properties this system has that might mislead a naive classifier. Each is a real property — just insufficient for G.

why_g_absent.description: Mechanistic argument for why G is absent. Not 'it is not alive' — something structural and falsifiable.

theory_failure_mode.description: What would a theory failure look like here? How would this get incorrectly promoted?

classification_risk.description: How likely a naive classifier is to incorrectly promote this case. high = most dangerous edge case.
